step.title: Receive Question and Choose Branch
step.goal: Receive the question from the caller, decide whether a LOGIC or UI prototype fits, and confirm with the user if the choice is ambiguous. The branch decision shapes everything in steps 2-5.

substep.title: Read Caller Context
substep.action:
The caller (ticket-grill or another grill-adopting skill) invoked this skill with context. Extract:
- The question being investigated (one sentence, from the turn focus) - The bundle directory path - The slug - Any prior findings already in the bundle's research file relevant to this prototype - The user's stated language/framework preferences if any
Store in session as {{session.caller_question}}, {{session.bundle_dir}}, {{session.slug}}, etc.

substep.title: Apply Branch Heuristics
substep.action:
Decide LOGIC vs UI:
**LOGIC branch fits when**: - The question concerns state transitions ("does X happen when Y?") - The question concerns data shape or computation ("what does the output look like when input is Z?") - The question concerns timing or concurrency ("does this race condition surface in practice?") - The question concerns integration ("does library X actually support method Y?") - The answer can be expressed in terminal output, JSON, or a structured log
**UI branch fits when**: - The question concerns layout or visual hierarchy - The question concerns interaction flow (clicks, navigation, transitions) - The question concerns information density or accessibility - The question concerns multiple visual variants the user wants to compare - The answer requires the user to LOOK at something to evaluate it
If both could apply, default to LOGIC — it is faster and produces more concrete evidence.
Store {{session.branch}}: "logic" or "ui".

substep.title: Choose Prototype Name
substep.action:
Slug the prototype with a short kebab-case name derived from the question: - 2-4 words - Names what is being tested, not the test method - Avoid generic names like "test" or "experiment"
Examples: - "Does Sequelize support nested transactions on Postgres?" -> sequelize-nested-tx - "What does a horizontal task list look like with 50 items?" -> horizontal-tasks-density - "Can we extract team_id from the JWT in middleware without a re-query?" -> jwt-team-extract
Store as {{session.prototype_name}}.
Validate: {{session.bundle_dir}}/prototypes/{{session.prototype_name}}/ does not already exist. If it does, append -2 / -3 until unique.

substep.title: Confirm Branch and Name

check.action: Halt the skill. Return {findings: null, prototype_path: null, cancelled: true} to the caller. ticket-grill will log the cancellation in its Q&A entry.

section.title: When neither branch fits
section.description: If a question genuinely cannot be answered by either branch (e.g., "will users adopt this feature?"), prototyping is the wrong tool. Such questions require user research, A/B testing, or beta rollout — none of which fit a throwaway-prototype session. Recommend to the caller that this question be deferred to a post-launch validation activity, not absorbed into ticket-writing Q↔R.

rules.rule: Refuse to prototype a full feature. Prototypes answer ONE question at a time. If the user asks for "a prototype of the whole team workspaces flow", split into multiple narrow prototypes or escalate to a spike ticket

Refuse to prototype code that should be production. If the question is "how would I implement X in production?", that is implementation work — not prototyping. Return findings: "use the actual implementation skill, not a throwaway prototype"
Refuse multi-day prototypes. Throwaway means 30-90 minutes typically; an hour or two at the outside. Anything longer becomes a spike ticket (kind: exploration tag)

section.title: Branch examples
section.example: Q: "Does the existing pagination middleware support cursor-based pagination?" Branch: LOGIC Why: Terminal-runnable; answer is yes/no with a code snippet showing how (or proving it can't).
Q: "What does the JWT payload look like after the new claims are added?" Branch: LOGIC Why: Terminal output suffices. Print the payload; done.
Q: "How does the team-switcher dropdown look with 50 teams vs 5 teams?" Branch: UI Why: Requires visual inspection. Two variants to compare.
Q: "Is the task-detail modal readable on mobile or does it need a different layout?" Branch: UI Why: Requires visual + interactive inspection at multiple viewports.
